pi_items.metadata_value: Based on EPA Clean Air Act enforcement action types documented in EPA ECHO database (echo.epa.gov)
pi_items.verified_value: EPA ECHO database exists at echo.epa.gov and documents real CAA enforcement cases; however, setup_task.sh creates synthetic representative cases, not pulling from real database
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed EPA ECHO is real (https://echo.epa.gov) and tracks CAA enforcement; setup_task.sh explicitly states 'Respondent names are representative; hearing dates adjusted for task scenario'

pi_items.metadata_value: Based on CMS administrative hearing types per 42 CFR Part 405 Subpart I Medicare appeals process
pi_items.verified_value: 42 CFR Part 405 Subpart I is real regulation governing Medicare appeals with ALJ hearings; CMS-OMHA (Office of Medicare Hearings and Appeals) is real entity; synthetic representative case created for task
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed 42 CFR Part 405 Subpart I exists and governs Medicare ALJ appeals (https://www.ecfr.gov); setup_task.sh creates synthetic representative case

pi_items.metadata_value: Based on real OAH (Office of Administrative Hearings) proceeding categories: Title IX and HIPAA enforcement are real ALJ matters
pi_items.verified_value: OAH offices exist in multiple states (California, Idaho, Maryland, Hawaii, Washington, DC, etc.); HIPAA enforcement through HHS administrative law judges is real; Title IX enforcement procedures are real
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed multiple state OAH offices exist; HHS Office for Civil Rights handles HIPAA enforcement with ALJ hearing procedures

pi_items.metadata_value: Referenced in required note text as 'ALJ-Admin Rule 4.2'
pi_items.source: Exhaustive web searches found no authoritative source documenting 'ALJ-Admin Rule 4.2'; similar 60-day deadlines and continuance procedures exist in various federal and state ALJ rules, but this specific rule designation not found in any official documentation

privileged_info_summary: The task involves 5 synthetic administrative hearing dockets (complaint cases) created via ArkCase API. Three cases are classified as overdue (hearing dates before 2025-12-30): Hargrove Industries (2025-10-15), Castellan Medical Group (2025-11-03), and Meridian Property Trust (2025-09-28). Two cases are current (after deadline): Thornfield Education Partners (2026-02-10) and Verity Healthcare Systems (2026-03-05). The required modifications for overdue cases are: set Priority to 'High', add note with specific text including reference to 'ALJ-Admin Rule 4.2', and set Status to 'In Progress'. The synthetic data is based on real administrative enforcement action types (EPA Clean Air Act, CMS Medicare appeals, OAH proceedings) and real regulatory frameworks (42 CFR 405 Subpart I, HIPAA, Title IX), though 'ALJ-Admin Rule 4.2' could not be verified as an actual published rule.
